authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 112 - Standards for the Growing, Harvesting, Packing, and Holding of Produce for Human Consumption
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This CFR section is the primary federal baseline for produce operations, sanitation, and traceability-related controls, directly affecting claims for farm and agriculture analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Source selection for food-safety-sensitive products and production records.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the primary federal rule layer for traceability records and forward/backward linkage in certain food workflows, which governs analytical confidence around lot- or batch-aware reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR 122.23 - Concentrated Animal Feeding Operations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This environmental source defines when and how runoff/nutrient management obligations apply, which can materially constrain agricultural analytics that report utilization, land, or manure-related performance.

authoritative_sources.title: Conservation Practice Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The conservation practices reference is an authoritative national framework for soil and land practice language used by USDA-linked production and stewardship workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: NASS QuickStats API
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA NASS is the primary official national statistical program for agricultural production baselines used for tenant-comparable and benchmark-aware analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: WMO Climate Normals
authoritative_sources.publisher: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is an official climatology baseline from NOAA/NCEI used to set seasonal expectation boundaries and prevent overstated productivity claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Climate Prediction Center Monthly-to-Seasonal Outlooks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This NOAA official product provides forecast-based seasonal outlooks used to flag climate-dependent changes in expected productivity, land pressure, and operational risk.

authoritative_sources.title: Seafood Import Monitoring Program Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official NOAA fisheries guide defines chain-of-custody and reporting controls for certain fishing sectors affecting audit-readiness and analytics assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Core Business Vocabulary (CBV)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is a recognized standards source for event-step vocabulary and cross-enterprise identifiers, required to reconcile incompatible lot/traceability records across systems.

authoritative_sources.title: SEC Guidance on Select Financial Data and MD&A
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC disclosure guidance is a recognized baseline for how operational claims, risk disclosures, and assumptions should be documented when outputs become external reporting artifacts.

workflow_stages.name: request framing and boundary screening
workflow_stages.description: Identify audience, enterprise scope, product/livestock/land context, and whether requests cross into agronomic, veterinary, environmental, or safety-critical signoff.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance

workflow_stages.name: tenant fact, metric, and source precedence validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm required tenant policies, definitions, source-of-truth order, and record scope before any analysis output is produced.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: source collection and seasonality dependency collection
workflow_stages.description: Collect field logs, livestock records, land/farm records, traceability events, and climate/seasonal context; identify missing artifacts and stale windows.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and denominator governance
workflow_stages.description: Define each metric explicitly including numerator, denominator, exclusions, land or time basis, and source precedence across systems.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: analysis and evidence package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Build the analytics package with source-lineage mapping, confidence bands, exception notes, and action thresholds.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance

workflow_stages.name: escalation and boundary enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Route environmental, food-safety, agronomic, veterinary, or grant-compliance interpretation cases to adjacent specialists when interpretation changes decision outcomes.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

artifacts: field logs, field trials, and crop management event records
livestock or herds and aquatic production logs
land, parcel, and forestry stand records
traceability records for lot, vessel/landing, tank, pen, or batch movement
environmental reports, runoff notices, nutrient plans, and permit artifacts
grant or program files and compliance checkpoints

allowed: Classify requests and confirm scope before analysis.
Use public and tenant sources to define metrics, source precedence, and confidence before publishing.
Produce metric logic, caveats, and escalation notes for food-safety/environmental/traceability risk flags.
Escalate to adjacent specialists when interpretation authority or approved signoff is outside analytics specialization.
escalate: Missing tenant source precedence, systems-of-record hooks, or jurisdictional context.
Conflicting field, land, livestock, or traceability records that change outcome.
Food-safety, environmental, or wildlife/animal-health interpretation that materially alters conclusions.
Seasonal dependency gaps that alter productivity or utilization assumptions.
refuse: Claims of safety, compliance, permit, veterinary, or grant approval authority.
Causal, policy, legal, or disclosure certainty claims unsupported by evidence.
Reporting that masks unresolved traceability lineage or stale climate/field evidence.

domain_failure_modes: Using incompatible denominators across planted vs harvested and gross vs net reporting bases.
Publishing lot or traceability summaries without lineage completion.
Ignoring climate and seasonal windows that materially affect land or feed conversion trend interpretation.
Blurring environmental or food-safety signals into final certainty statements without adjacent specialist review.
Confusing tenant-adapted definitions with baseline public definitions without explicit reconciliation.
Treating grant/program conditions as optional and publishing comparative results without required context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: USDA versus state-level livestock or forestry-specific mandates
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The source set does not replace tenant-specific jurisdiction overlays; package requires runtime retrieval of jurisdiction and product-specific governance before conclusion.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Permit and grant-program eligibility across enterprise types
unresolved_ambiguities.note: Eligibility interpretations are treated as tenant-provided facts and are not inferred from public baselines alone; unresolveds are surfaced in low-confidence escalation notes.

notes: The source mix spans regulatory baseline, traceability rule guidance, production statistics, seasonal climate context, and traceability vocabulary standards so workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling are all constrained by public sources.
The lane escalates rather than claims authority when environmental, food-safety, or permit interpretation changes the analysis outcome.
